This article is about the mixed martial artist. For the skateboarder, see [Tony Ferguson (skateboarder)](/wiki/Tony_Ferguson_(skateboarder) "Tony Ferguson (skateboarder)").

**Anthony Armand Ferguson Padilla**[[7]](#cite_note-7) (born February 12, 1984) is an American professional [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") and [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxing "Professional boxing"), and is the current [MFB](/wiki/Misfits_Boxing "Misfits Boxing") Middleweight Champion. As a mixed martial artist, he is a former [Interim UFC Lightweight Champion](/wiki/UFC_Lightweight_Champion "UFC Lightweight Champion")[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) A professional since 2008, and was signed to the [UFC](/wiki/UFC "UFC") from his *[The Ultimate Fighter 13](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Lesnar_vs._Team_dos_Santos "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Lesnar vs. Team dos Santos")* win in 2011 until his departure in 2025. He is regarded as one of the greatest fighters to not gain an undisputed belt.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) He has trained in [freestyle wrestling](/wiki/Freestyle_wrestling "Freestyle wrestling"), [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing"), [jiu-jitsu](/wiki/Jiu-jitsu "Jiu-jitsu"),[[4]](#cite_note-ufcespanol-4) [Muay Thai](/wiki/Muay_Thai "Muay Thai"),[[12]](#cite_note-12) and [Wing Chun](/wiki/Wing_Chun "Wing Chun").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Ferguson was born in [Oxnard, California](/wiki/Oxnard,_California "Oxnard, California"),[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) on February 12, 1984, but grew up mainly in [Muskegon, Michigan](/wiki/Muskegon,_Michigan "Muskegon, Michigan"). He is of [Mexican](/wiki/Mexican_Americans "Mexican Americans") heritage. His surname of Ferguson comes from his [Scottish American](/wiki/Scottish_Americans "Scottish Americans") stepfather.[[16]](#cite_note-UF-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)

Ferguson was a three-sport athlete at [Muskegon Catholic Central High School](/wiki/Muskegon_Catholic_Central_High_School "Muskegon Catholic Central High School") in American football, baseball, and [wrestling](/wiki/Wrestling "Wrestling"). He was a starting defensive back for the 2000 Division 8 state football champions and was a three-time All-State selection in wrestling, winning the 152-lb division in 2002.

After high school, Ferguson enrolled at [Central Michigan University](/wiki/Central_Michigan_University "Central Michigan University") before transferring to [Grand Valley State University](/wiki/Grand_Valley_State_University "Grand Valley State University"). He also did a stint at [Muskegon Community College](/wiki/Muskegon_Community_College "Muskegon Community College"). He did not complete his degree, but had a successful collegiate wrestling career, winning the 2006 [National Collegiate Wrestling Association](/wiki/National_Collegiate_Wrestling_Association "National Collegiate Wrestling Association") national wrestling championship in the 165-lb division.[[6]](#cite_note-GVSUwrestling-6)

Following college, Ferguson moved back to California to be closer to his extended family, working in marketing and sales during the day and picking up shifts as a bartender at night. One night, while working a bar shift, a patron noticed Ferguson's [cauliflower ear](/wiki/Cauliflower_ear "Cauliflower ear") and struck up a conversation about his wrestling background. The patron was a trainer at a local MMA gym and invited him to work with some young [mixed martial artists](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") on their wrestling. Shortly after this, he decided to pursue a professional mixed martial arts (MMA) career.

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

Ferguson began his professional MMA career fighting in small organizations around California in 2007. Notable matches in his early career include a win over kickboxing champion [Joe Schilling](/wiki/Joe_Schilling "Joe Schilling") and a loss to future [World Extreme Cagefighting](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting") (WEC) fighter [Karen Darabedyan](/wiki/Karen_Darabedyan "Karen Darabedyan").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

### *The Ultimate Fighter*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: The Ultimate Fighter")]

Ferguson applied to compete in the UFC's reality series *The Ultimate Fighter* multiple times. In 2010, he was finally accepted to compete in season 13 of the series after amassing a 10–2 professional record and winning the welterweight championship in PureCombat. He competed as a welterweight on *[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Lesnar vs. Team dos Santos](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Lesnar_vs._Team_dos_Santos "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Lesnar vs. Team dos Santos")*.

Ferguson was selected as the third pick for Team Lesnar. In his first fight, Ferguson defeated [Justin Edwards](/wiki/Justin_Edwards_(fighter) "Justin Edwards (fighter)") by knockout in the first round. He next faced Ryan McGillivray in the quarterfinals and won by a TKO in the first round. He then faced Chuck O'Neil in the semifinals and won by TKO in the third round to advance to the final.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Ferguson officially made his UFC debut at [the Ultimate Fighter 13 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_13_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter 13 Finale") against [Ramsey Nijem](/wiki/Ramsey_Nijem "Ramsey Nijem") to determine the winner of [The Ultimate Fighter 13](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_13 "The Ultimate Fighter 13"). Ferguson defeated Nijem by knockout in the first round to win a UFC contract.[[20]](#cite_note-20) He also received a $40,000 *Knockout of the Night* bonus.[[21]](#cite_note-TUF13-21)

Following his debut, Ferguson returned to lightweight and faced [Aaron Riley](/wiki/Aaron_Riley "Aaron Riley") on September 24, 2011, at [UFC 135](/wiki/UFC_135 "UFC 135"). The fight was stopped after the first round after Riley said he had a broken jaw, resulting in a TKO victory for Ferguson.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

Ferguson then faced MMA veteran [Yves Edwards](/wiki/Yves_Edwards "Yves Edwards") at [the Ultimate Fighter 14 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_14_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter 14 Finale").[[23]](#cite_note-23) Ferguson won via unanimous decision (30–27, 30–27, and 29–28).[[24]](#cite_note-24)

Ferguson was expected to face [Dennis Hallman](/wiki/Dennis_Hallman "Dennis Hallman") on May 5, 2012, at [UFC on Fox: Diaz vs. Miller](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox:_Diaz_vs._Miller "UFC on Fox: Diaz vs. Miller").[[25]](#cite_note-25) Hallman pulled out of the bout with an injury and replaced by [Thiago Tavares](/wiki/Thiago_Tavares "Thiago Tavares").[[26]](#cite_note-26) Then, Tavares was forced out of the bout with an injury and replaced by [Michael Johnson](/wiki/Michael_Johnson_(fighter) "Michael Johnson (fighter)").[[27]](#cite_note-27) Ferguson lost the fight by unanimous decision.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

#### Win streak

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Win streak")]

After over a year away from competition while healing an arm injury incurred in his last fight, Ferguson returned to face [Mike Rio](/wiki/Mike_Rio "Mike Rio") on October 19, 2013, at [UFC 166](/wiki/UFC_166 "UFC 166") which was the beginning of his twelve fight win streak.[[29]](#cite_note-29) He won by D'Arce choke submission in the first round. The win also earned him his first *Submission of the Night* award, valued at $60,000.[[30]](#cite_note-UFC166-30)

Ferguson faced [Katsunori Kikuno](/wiki/Katsunori_Kikuno "Katsunori Kikuno") on May 24, 2014, at [UFC 173](/wiki/UFC_173 "UFC 173").[[31]](#cite_note-31) He won the fight by knockout in the first round.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

Ferguson was expected to face [Danny Castillo](/wiki/Danny_Castillo "Danny Castillo") on August 2, 2014, at [UFC 176](/wiki/UFC_176 "UFC 176").[[33]](#cite_note-33) After [UFC 176](/wiki/UFC_176 "UFC 176") was cancelled, Castillo/Ferguson was rescheduled and took place on August 30, 2014, at [UFC 177](/wiki/UFC_177 "UFC 177").[[34]](#cite_note-34) Ferguson won the fight via split decision.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

Ferguson faced [Abel Trujillo](/wiki/Abel_Trujillo "Abel Trujillo") on December 6, 2014, at [UFC 181](/wiki/UFC_181 "UFC 181").[[36]](#cite_note-36) Ferguson won the bout in the second round by submission.[[37]](#cite_note-37)

Ferguson was expected to face [Yancy Medeiros](/wiki/Yancy_Medeiros "Yancy Medeiros") on February 28, 2015, at [UFC 184](/wiki/UFC_184 "UFC 184").[[38]](#cite_note-38) Medeiros pulled out of the bout, citing injury, and was replaced by [Gleison Tibau](/wiki/Gleison_Tibau "Gleison Tibau").[[39]](#cite_note-39) Ferguson won the fight by submission in the first round which also earned him a *Performance of the Night* bonus.[[40]](#cite_note-UFC184-40)

Ferguson faced [Josh Thomson](/wiki/Josh_Thomson "Josh Thomson") on July 15, 2015, at [UFC Fight Night 71](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_71 "UFC Fight Night 71").[[41]](#cite_note-41) He won the fight via unanimous decision (30–27, 30–27, and 30–26) and earned his second straight *Performance of the Night* bonus.[[42]](#cite_note-42)[[43]](#cite_note-UFCFN71-43)

Ferguson was expected to face [Khabib Nurmagomedov](/wiki/Khabib_Nurmagomedov "Khabib Nurmagomedov") on December 11, 2015, at [the Ultimate Fighter 22 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_McGregor_vs._Team_Faber#The_Ultimate_Fighter_22_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Team McGregor vs. Team Faber").[[44]](#cite_note-44) Nurmagomedov pulled out of the fight in late October, citing injury, and was replaced by [Edson Barboza](/wiki/Edson_Barboza "Edson Barboza").[[45]](#cite_note-45) After a back-and-forth first round that had Ferguson docked one point due to an illegal upkick, Ferguson finished Barboza with a D'Arce choke submission in the second round.[[46]](#cite_note-46) He earned his third consecutive *Performance of the Night* bonus as well as a *Fight of the Night* bonus.[[47]](#cite_note-Tristen_Critchfield-47)

A rematch with Michael Johnson was briefly scheduled for March 5, 2016, at [UFC 196](/wiki/UFC_196 "UFC 196").[[48]](#cite_note-48) On January 27, it was announced that Johnson withdrew from the bout due to injury. In turn, the bout with Nurmagomedov was rescheduled and expected take place on April 16, 2016, at [UFC on Fox 19](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_19 "UFC on Fox 19").[[49]](#cite_note-49) On April 5, Ferguson pulled out of the bout due to a [lung](/wiki/Lung "Lung") issue.[[50]](#cite_note-50) He was replaced by promotional newcomer [Darrell Horcher](/wiki/Darrell_Horcher "Darrell Horcher").[[51]](#cite_note-51)

Ferguson was expected to face [Michael Chiesa](/wiki/Michael_Chiesa "Michael Chiesa") on July 13, 2016, at [UFC Fight Night 91](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_91 "UFC Fight Night 91").[[52]](#cite_note-52) Chiesa pulled out of the fight on June 27, citing injury, and was replaced by promotional newcomer [Lando Vannata](/wiki/Lando_Vannata "Lando Vannata").[[53]](#cite_note-53)[[54]](#cite_note-54) Ferguson won his second straight fight via D'Arce choke and was awarded another *Fight of the Night* bonus.[[55]](#cite_note-Ben_Fowlkes-55)[[56]](#cite_note-56)

Ferguson fought former champion [Rafael dos Anjos](/wiki/Rafael_dos_Anjos "Rafael dos Anjos") on November 5, 2016, at [the Ultimate Fighter Latin America 3 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Latin_America_3#The_Ultimate_Fighter_Latin_America_3_Finale:_dos_Anjos_vs._Ferguson "The Ultimate Fighter: Latin America 3").[[57]](#cite_note-57) He won the fight by unanimous decision. Both participants were awarded *Fight of the Night*, which gave Ferguson his sixth post-fight bonus in his past five fights.[[58]](#cite_note-Damon_Martin-58)[[59]](#cite_note-59)

The pairing with Nurmagomedov was scheduled for a third time at [UFC 209](/wiki/UFC_209 "UFC 209"), this time for the interim UFC Lightweight Championship.[[60]](#cite_note-60) Prior to the weigh-ins for the event, Nurmagomedov was hospitalized due to the ill effects of his [weight cut](/wiki/Weight_cutting "Weight cutting"), and the bout was cancelled yet again.[[61]](#cite_note-61)

Ferguson faced [Kevin Lee](/wiki/Kevin_Lee_(fighter) "Kevin Lee (fighter)") on October 7, 2017, at [UFC 216](/wiki/UFC_216 "UFC 216") for the interim UFC Lightweight Championship. He won the bout by triangle choke submission in the third round.[[62]](#cite_note-62)[[63]](#cite_note-63)
A bout with Nurmagomedov had been scheduled for the fourth time on April 7, 2018, at [UFC 223](/wiki/UFC_223 "UFC 223").[[64]](#cite_note-64) On April 1, 2018, Ferguson reportedly tore his [fibular collateral ligament](/wiki/Fibular_collateral_ligament "Fibular collateral ligament") while walking on the set for a pre-fight media obligation and was forced to withdraw from the event.[[65]](#cite_note-65)[[66]](#cite_note-66)

Ferguson faced [Anthony Pettis](/wiki/Anthony_Pettis "Anthony Pettis") on October 6, 2018, at [UFC 229](/wiki/UFC_229 "UFC 229").[[67]](#cite_note-67) He won the bout via corner stoppage after two rounds. Pettis' longtime cornerman and coach, [Duke Roufus](/wiki/Duke_Roufus "Duke Roufus"), informed the referee that he would not continue after suffering a broken hand.[[68]](#cite_note-68) Their performance earned both participants [*Fight of the Night*](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") honors.[[69]](#cite_note-MMA_News-69)

Ferguson next faced [Donald Cerrone](/wiki/Donald_Cerrone "Donald Cerrone") on June 8, 2019, at [UFC 238](/wiki/UFC_238 "UFC 238").[[70]](#cite_note-70) He won the fight via TKO due to a doctor stoppage after Cerrone blew his nose between rounds 2 & 3 which caused his swollen right eye to completely close, rendering him unable to continue.[[71]](#cite_note-71) This fight earned both participants [*Fight of the Night*](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") honors.[[72]](#cite_note-mmajunkie.com-72)

#### Losing streak

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Losing streak")]

Ferguson was scheduled to face [Khabib Nurmagomedov](/wiki/Khabib_Nurmagomedov "Khabib Nurmagomedov") for the [UFC Lightweight Championship](/wiki/UFC_Lightweight_Championship "UFC Lightweight Championship") on April 18, 2020, at [UFC 249](/wiki/UFC_249 "UFC 249"), however the fight was canceled after Nurmagomedov was unable to leave Russia due to restricted air travel as a result of the [coronavirus pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[73]](#cite_note-73) Ferguson then agreed to compete for the Interim UFC Lightweight Championship against a late replacement in [Justin Gaethje](/wiki/Justin_Gaethje "Justin Gaethje") at the event.[[74]](#cite_note-74) On April 9, UFC president [Dana White](/wiki/Dana_White "Dana White") announced that this event was postponed[[75]](#cite_note-Postpone-75) and the bout eventually took place on May 9, 2020.[[76]](#cite_note-76) He was outstruck by Gaethje for all five rounds, eventually losing the fight via TKO in the fifth round.[[77]](#cite_note-77) This fight earned both participants [*Fight of the Night*](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") honors.[[78]](#cite_note-:0-78)

Ferguson faced [Charles Oliveira](/wiki/Charles_Oliveira "Charles Oliveira") on December 12, 2020, at [UFC 256](/wiki/UFC_256 "UFC 256").[[79]](#cite_note-79) He lost the fight via unanimous decision after being dominated and controlled on the ground for most of the fight.[[80]](#cite_note-80)

Ferguson faced [Beneil Dariush](/wiki/Beneil_Dariush "Beneil Dariush") on May 15, 2021, at [UFC 262](/wiki/UFC_262 "UFC 262").[[81]](#cite_note-81) He lost the fight via unanimous decision being once again controlled on the ground for the majority of the fight.[[82]](#cite_note-82)

Ferguson faced former [Bellator Lightweight Champion](/wiki/Bellator_Lightweight_Champion "Bellator Lightweight Champion") [Michael Chandler](/wiki/Michael_Chandler "Michael Chandler") on May 7, 2022 at [UFC 274](/wiki/UFC_274 "UFC 274").[[83]](#cite_note-83) Despite a strong first round, Ferguson lost the fight via knockout early in the second round.[[84]](#cite_note-84)

Ferguson was scheduled to return to the welterweight division to face [Li Jingliang](/wiki/Li_Jingliang "Li Jingliang") on September 10, 2022, in the co-main event of [UFC 279](/wiki/UFC_279 "UFC 279").[[85]](#cite_note-85) However, Ferguson instead moved up to the main event to face [Nate Diaz](/wiki/Nate_Diaz "Nate Diaz"), after his original opponent [Khamzat Chimaev](/wiki/Khamzat_Chimaev "Khamzat Chimaev") was pulled from their fight due to missing weight.[[86]](#cite_note-86) Ferguson lost the fight via a guillotine choke submission in the fourth round.[[87]](#cite_note-87)

Ferguson returned to the lightweight division to face [King Green](/wiki/King_Green "King Green") at [UFC 291](/wiki/UFC_291 "UFC 291") on July 29, 2023.[[88]](#cite_note-88) He lost the fight via technical submission due to an arm-triangle choke in the final seconds of the third round.[[89]](#cite_note-89)

Ferguson faced [Paddy Pimblett](/wiki/Paddy_Pimblett "Paddy Pimblett") on December 16, 2023, at [UFC 296](/wiki/UFC_296 "UFC 296").[[90]](#cite_note-90) He lost the fight via unanimous decision.[[91]](#cite_note-91)

Ferguson faced [Michael Chiesa](/wiki/Michael_Chiesa "Michael Chiesa") in a welterweight bout on August 3, 2024 at [UFC on ABC 7](/wiki/UFC_on_ABC_7 "UFC on ABC 7").[[92]](#cite_note-92) He lost the fight via a rear-naked choke submission in the first round.[[93]](#cite_note-93) With this eighth consecutive loss, Ferguson surpassed [B.J. Penn](/wiki/B.J._Penn "B.J. Penn") and now holds the record for the longest losing streak in UFC history.[[94]](#cite_note-losingstreak-94)

### Global Fight League

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Global Fight League")]

On January 24, 2025, it was announced that Ferguson had parted ways with the UFC and signed with the Global Fight League.[[95]](#cite_note-95)

Ferguson was drafted by the GFL's Team Los Angeles the same day his signing was announced.[[96]](#cite_note-96) He was scheduled to face [Dillon Danis](/wiki/Dillon_Danis "Dillon Danis") on May 25, 2025 at GFL 2.[[97]](#cite_note-97) However, the first two GFL events were postponed indefinitely.[[98]](#cite_note-98)

## Boxing career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Boxing career")]

In January 2025, Ferguson was among names for a potential boxing bout against English megastar [KSI](/wiki/KSI "KSI") after his bout with English [footballer](/wiki/Footballer "Footballer") [Wayne Bridge](/wiki/Wayne_Bridge "Wayne Bridge") fell through.[[99]](#cite_note-99) Ferguson revealed he turned down the offer to face [Dillon Danis](/wiki/Dillon_Danis "Dillon Danis") on the then announced GFL event in May.[[100]](#cite_note-100)

### Ferguson vs Papi

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Ferguson vs Papi")]

Main article: [Tony Ferguson vs Salt Papi](/wiki/Tony_Ferguson_vs_Salt_Papi "Tony Ferguson vs Salt Papi")

On July 5, 2025, Ferguson stated that he had a bout in boxing scheduled for August 30.[[101]](#cite_note-101) Two weeks later, it was announced that Ferguson would face Filipino [influencer](/wiki/Influencer "Influencer") Salt Papi for the MFB interim [middleweight](/wiki/Middleweight "Middleweight") title, headlining [Misfits 22 – Ring of Thrones](/wiki/Tony_Ferguson_vs_Salt_Papi "Tony Ferguson vs Salt Papi") alongside [Darren Till](/wiki/Darren_Till "Darren Till") vs [Luke Rockhold](/wiki/Luke_Rockhold "Luke Rockhold") at the [Manchester Arena](/wiki/Manchester_Arena "Manchester Arena") in [Manchester](/wiki/Manchester "Manchester"), England.[[102]](#cite_note-102) Ferguson won the championship by technical knockout in the third round.[[103]](#cite_note-103)

### Ferguson vs Spencer

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Ferguson vs Spencer")]

On November 4, [Misfits Boxing](/wiki/Misfits_Boxing "Misfits Boxing") co-president [Mams Taylor](/wiki/Mams_Taylor "Mams Taylor") announced that Ferguson would be returning to the ring in December to unify his interim title on the [Misfits Mania – The Fight Before Christmas](/wiki/Andrew_Tate_vs_Chase_DeMoor "Andrew Tate vs Chase DeMoor") undercard.[[104]](#cite_note-104) On November 19, the bout was announced for December 20 at the [Dubai Duty Free Tennis Stadium](/wiki/Aviation_Club_Tennis_Centre "Aviation Club Tennis Centre") in [Dubai](/wiki/Dubai "Dubai"), UAE against Isle of Man influencer Warren Spencer.[[105]](#cite_note-105) On November 22, Taylor confirmed during an [X Space](/wiki/Twitter_Spaces "Twitter Spaces") that the MFB middleweight champion, Saudi influencer AnEsonGib, had relinquish his title therefore making his bout with Spencer now set for the vacant MFB middleweight championship.[[106]](#cite_note-106) He defeated Spencer via unanimous decision (49–45, 49–45, 48–46).[[107]](#cite_note-107)[[108]](#cite_note-108)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Ferguson has two sons with his wife, Cristina Ferguson.[[109]](#cite_note-109) In March 2019, Cristina filed a temporary restraining order against Tony in Orange County, California, alleging uncharacteristic behavior including severe paranoia, prolonged sleep deprivation, destruction of their home fireplace, and beliefs that a tracking chip had been inserted into his leg during reconstructive knee surgery.[[110]](#cite_note-:1-110)[[111]](#cite_note-111) According to court filings reported by multiple media outlets, she also stated that Ferguson had taken their son from the family home for several days without her consent, and that police had been called to their residence on multiple occasions over the preceding months.[[112]](#cite_note-112)

She did not allege physical abuse, and later stated that the filing was intended as a precautionary measure to encourage him to seek help for his mental state.[[110]](#cite_note-:1-110) By April 2019, she had withdrawn the request, and the restraining order expired without any criminal charges being filed.[[113]](#cite_note-113)[[114]](#cite_note-114)[[115]](#cite_note-115) He then began to resume MMA.[[116]](#cite_note-116)

In the early hours of May 7, 2023, Ferguson was arrested in [Hollywood](/wiki/Hollywood,_Los_Angeles "Hollywood, Los Angeles"), [California](/wiki/California "California") and charged with [driving under the influence](/wiki/Driving_under_the_influence "Driving under the influence") after allegedly crashing his pickup truck into two other vehicles outside of a nightclub, causing his truck to roll over in the process. He was taken into custody and booked into the LAPD Hollywood police station where his bail was set at $30,000.[[117]](#cite_note-117) He was later released from custody on a [personal recognizance](/wiki/Recognizance "Recognizance") bond.[[118]](#cite_note-118)

## Fighting style

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Fighting style")]

Ferguson is known for his well-rounded style which includes unorthodox striking, exceptional cardio, sharp elbows, and proficiency in D'Arce chokes. During his peak, he achieved a 12-fight winning streak, finishing nine of his opponents. His aggressive fighting style, particularly his use of elbows, often left opponents bloodied and contributed to six "Fight of the Night" bonuses.[[119]](#cite_note-119)

## Public appearance and cultural influences

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Ferguson&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Public appearance and cultural influences")]

Ferguson is known as one of MMA's most eccentric personalities.[[120]](#cite_note-120) He constructed his own custom training apparatus at his house in [Big Bear Lake, California](/wiki/Big_Bear_Lake,_California "Big Bear Lake, California") and regularly posts videos of his unique workouts on his personal [Instagram](/wiki/Instagram "Instagram") page. These include using a Wing Chun dummy,[[121]](#cite_note-121) smacking his arms and legs against metal poles to build bone strength, throwing baseballs off a pitcher's mound to build power for right-hand punches, swinging a hammer while balanced on a gym ball, and somersault jumps over tall stacks of gym mats.[[122]](#cite_note-122)[[123]](#cite_note-123) A lot of people including fellow fighters have regularly commented on these unorthodox routines, and other fighters such as [Belal Muhammad](/wiki/Belal_Muhammad_(fighter) "Belal Muhammad (fighter)") have even parodied Ferguson's training videos on their own personal social media pages or exaggerated them.[[124]](#cite_note-124) Ferguson has written on his [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter") account that he eschews sparring in training as he "finds it limits my creativity" when fighting.[[125]](#cite_note-125)
